title: arctium lappa subsp. pubens
intro: Arctium lappa subsp. pubens, also known as wild burdock or great burdock, is a subspecies of the Arctium lappa plant, a biennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the lymphatic system and promote detoxification, making it beneficial for skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Wild burdock root has been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, arthritis, and even cancer, and is often consumed as a tea, tincture, or supplement. Its root and leaves can also be used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function.
